ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL SIX CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL SIX CAUSES OF ACTION. PER PUBLIC UTILITIES CODE 1759(A), THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN THIS ACTION. WRITTEN ORDER SIGNED. (SEE FORMAL ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, E-MAIL: MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG (L&M302)

Motion To Reconsider And Vacate Order Sustaining Demurrer
Off Calendar Mar-12-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joel B. Crane, Esq. 415-848-8913 of Gough & Hancock for Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG's Motion To Reconsider And Vacate Order Sustaining Demurrer.

Plaintiff Jordan Rosenberg's motion for reconsideration of the January 22, 2018 order sustaining the demurrer of defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company is denied. The motion does not identify any new law, facts or circumstances that warrant reconsideration. The motion also fails to include a declaration in compliance with CCP 1008(a). Mr. Rosenberg's moving papers suggest that he was blindsided at the hearing by the court's reference to Lefebvre v. Southern California Edison (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 143, but that case was cited in PG&E's moving and reply papers on the demurrer. Notwithstanding that there is no basis for reconsideration, the court did another review of PG&E's demurrer and remains of the view that the demurrer was correctly decided.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To Mar-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jordan Rosenberg (415-999-9999) appears as self-represented plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Joel B. Crane of the law offices of Gough & Hancock (415-848-8913) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to all six causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Jordan Rosenberg is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to all six causes of action. Per Public Utilities Code 1759(a), this court lacks jurisdiction to entertain this action. The parties are familiar with the three-part test used to determine whether a superior court lacks jurisdiction to hear an action against a utility regulated by the California Public Utilities Commission. The first element of that test is satisfied because the California Constitution gives the CPUC broad authority to adopt policies pertaining to overcharges. (Hartwell Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 256, 264.) The second element is satisfied because Electric Rule No. 8 governs the termination of services and Electric Rules No. 10 and 17.1 provide for a detailed procedure regarding the review of bills. The 48-hour notice that Mr. Rosenberg attached to his complaint expressly refers to the CPUC billing review process. The third element is satisfied because any relief afforded by this court would interfere with the CPUC's exercise of its regulatory authority to resolve billing disputes. Mr. Rosenberg's labeling of his claims as fraud and civil extortion does not support jurisdiction in this court. What matters is the substance of the claims, not their labels. The substance of Mr. Rosenberg's claims is that PG&E committed actionable misconduct by demanding that Mr. Rosenberg pay the amount that PG&E asserted was owed to it and, absent compliance, PG&E threatened to disconnect its utility service to Mr. Rosenberg's residence. This substance falls within the core of CPUC's regulatory authority to determine proper procedures for a regulated utility to seek payment for its services and the circumstances that permit the regulated utility to disconnect its service due to asserted non-payment.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: mtorreano@sftc.org (L&M302)
